commit.message:
etcd - fix: support grpc-gateway v2 error format for etcd 3.6 compatibility (#1964)

* etcd - fix: support grpc-gateway v2 error format for etcd 3.6 compatibility

Moving the etcd docker image to the latest release (the 3.6 line) made the
@keyv/etcd suite fail on "EtcdClient surfaces error responses from etcd".

etcd 3.6 upgraded its JSON/HTTP gateway from grpc-gateway v1 to v2. v2
dropped the top-level `error` string and now reports failures using the
google.rpc.Status shape (`code` / `message` / `details`):

  3.5: {"error":"etcdserver: requested lease not found","code":5,"message":"..."}
  3.6: {"code":5,"message":"etcdserver: requested lease not found"}

The in-house client read only `parsed.error`, so on 3.6 it threw the generic
"etcd request failed: 404 Not Found" and lost the real message. Every other
endpoint (range/put/lease/deleterange) is byte-for-byte compatible between
3.5 and 3.6, so this was the only failure.

The client now reads `error` (etcd <3.6) and falls back to `message`
(etcd >=3.6), preserving the real error text across all versions. Added two
deterministic tests that stub fetch with each shape so both paths are covered
regardless of which etcd version backs the live suite. Bumped the docker-compose
pins from 3.5.15-0 to the latest 3.6.12-0.

Verified end to end against etcd 3.5.15-0, 3.6.1-0 and 3.6.12-0: 107/107 pass.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HQtXXVhuZnu9hC12Ac7UNV

* etcd - test: use vi.stubGlobal for fetch mocking in error-format tests

Addresses review feedback: replace the direct globalThis.fetch reassignment
in the cross-version error tests with Vitest's vi.stubGlobal /
vi.unstubAllGlobals, which is the idiomatic, side-effect-safe way to mock
globals and avoids leaking the stub into other tests.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01HQtXXVhuZnu9hC12Ac7UNV

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@ services:
     ports:
       - "11211:11211"
   keyv_etcd:
-    image: registry.k8s.io/etcd:3.5.15-0
+    image: registry.k8s.io/etcd:3.6.12-0
     platform: linux/arm64/v8
     command:
       - etcd

@@ -88,7 +88,7 @@ services:
     ports:
       - "11211:11211"
   keyv_etcd:
-    image: registry.k8s.io/etcd:3.5.15-0
+    image: registry.k8s.io/etcd:3.6.12-0
     command:
       - etcd
       - --listen-client-urls=http://0.0.0.0:2379
